channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sonos outlines a plan to win back user trust after its app fiasco
item.description: Speaker company Sonos laid out a number of new plans and commitments on Tuesday in an attempt to turn around the beleaguered sound company following a bug-riddled app relaunch in May that’s still impacting the company.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: US port strike has CIOs prepping for supply chain implications
item.description: Dockworkers have launched a widespread strike at ports across the eastern US from Texas to Maine, with wages and automation topping their list of labor concerns. The stoppage, the first in decades, could cause price hikes and supply shortages, depending on how long it lasts, the AP reports.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Docker API Exploited by Hackers for Cryptojacking Attacks - A Growing Concern
item.description: Hackers have been exploiting Docker Engine APIs to target containers with cryptojackers and malware, highlighting the need for proper protection and security measures.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Donald Trump’s crypto venture begins sign-ups while business purpose remains unclear
item.description: World Liberty is just the latest of Trump’s crypto efforts. The Republican presidential nominee has made supporting crypto a part of his platform as he seeks reelection in November.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: JNUC 2024: Jamf brings AI and more to Apple enterprise
item.description: Apple management and security services provider Jamf unveiled a series of tools and features to support Apple admins at its well-attended JNUC event Oct. 1, with a focus on AI, security, and all-new solutions to make it easier to manage large Apple fleets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Offshore Wind Farm in New Jersey Clears Key Approval
item.description: The federal government has given its approval to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, but concerns remain over the potential impact on local communities and environmental concerns.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Waymo self-driving car interrupts Vice President Harris’ motorcade in San Francisco
item.description: Harris, the Democratic presidential nominee, was in the city staying overnight Friday at the Fairmont Hotel before a campaign fundraiser at the San Francisco Palace of Fine Arts Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kristin J. Bender, The Mercury News

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas has sued YouTube and its parent company Alphabet, saying the video-sharing platform is deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Salesforce: Latest news and insights
item.description: Salesforce is a vendor of cloud-based software and applications for sales, customer service, marketing automation, ecommerce, analytics, and application development. Based in San Francisco, Calif.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Making use of command history on Linux
item.description: Command history on Linux allows you to review and repeat previous commands with little effort. It is built into the bash shell (included in the shell executable).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft details Windows Recall security and privacy features
item.description: Microsoft has outlined plans to secure its controversial Windows Recall feature ahead of launch. Microsoft unveiled Recall in May, pitching it as an “explorable timeline of your PC’s past.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: California Governor Newsom vetoes AI safety bill, arguing it’s ‘not the best approach’
item.description: In a significant move, California Governor Gavin Newsom vetoed a highly contested artificial intelligence (AI) safety bill on Sunday, citing concerns over its potential to stifle innovation and impose excessive restrictions on AI technologies, including basic functions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How AI is transforming business today
item.description: Hannah Calhoon, vice president of AI for Indeed, uses artificial intelligence “to make existing tasks faster, easier, higher quality and more effective.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump posts, then deletes, fake image of Kamala Harris with Diddy
item.description: The man in the real image taken in 2001 is talk show host Montel Williams, whom Harris dated at that time.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft adds safety tools to Azure AI Studio
item.description: Now that the initial euphoria about generative AI’s capabilities has worn off, reality is setting in: Large language models without appropriate safeguards can be remarkably naïve and are all too willing to share all they know.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot rollouts slowed by data security, ROI concerns
item.description: With the promise of greater office worker productivity, Microsoft 365 Copilot generated a lot of business interest even before it launched last November. (It was also for a while renamed Copilot for Microsoft 365; that changed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Intel and the US set to seal $8.5B CHIPS Act funding
item.description: Intel is on the verge of finalizing an $8.5 billion funding deal with the US government, under the CHIPS and Science Act, as part of an effort to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing amid growing global competition, a Financial Times report said.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service
item.description: House Homeland Security Committee Chairman Mark Green's cyber workforce bill adds to a growing push to embrace skills-based hiring and training for cyber jobs. The post House cyber workforce bill pushes two-year degrees for gov service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge
item.description: "Inside that synthetic version of the code, we inserted vulnerabilities for the competitors to find," AI Cyber Challenge program manager Andrew Carney said. The post DARPA wraps up semifinal of artificial intelligence challenge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: CrowdStrike executive apologizes to Congress for July global tech outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An executive at cybersecurity company CrowdStrike apologized in testimony to Congress for sparking a global technology outage over the summer. “We let our customers down,” said Adam Meyers, who leads CrowdStrike's threat intelligence division, in a hearing before a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
